programmes.show_description: The unsparing panel show that puts the mockers on the world's headline-makers. This 'best of' end-of-series special also features riotous new material and daft outtakes.
Star names turn 'true detective' in the mirthful fact-or-fiction panel show. Jo Brand, Roisin Conaty, Paul Foot and Ray Mears join Lee Mack, David Mitchell and Rob Brydon.
Upsadaisy: Sandi Toksvig hosts an 'upsadaisy' episode and is joined by Justin Moorhouse, Emmanuel Sonubi, Holly Walsh and Alan Davies.
Fly-on-the-wall comedy. Ellen has to move the green room downstairs so it's accessible for headliner Tim Renkow, but George hasn't got the memo and has given the space to a bingo club.
Fly-on-the-wall comedy. An estate agent tells George that selling up would make him enormously wealthy - it looks like the writing's on the wall, but can the gang do anything about it?
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Breakfast: The Hairy Bikers look for great ways to kick start the day. They find out about how our morning meal has changed and evolved through the years.
Surrey police investigate a drug-dealing gang, farmers in Northamptonshire are targeted by a gang in the countryside, and a crime squad in Kent investigate residential burglaries.
Special police task forces tackle a hit-and-run in Lancashire which turns out to be a deliberate attack on a single mum, and they also tackle a drug-dealing family dynasty in Bury.
All Aboard an Australian Icon: The Ghan's maintenance team must race to ensure this iconic train departs the next day, and a routine job on a Queensland Rail commuter train hits a snag.

programmes.show_name: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programmes.show_description: On the Cesme Peninsula, Rick Stein enjoys creamy Armola cheese, Urla's famous red wine, and the ultimate Turkish breakfast.
120 kilograms of cocaine are discovered in the axles of a lowboy trailer, and two men are caught attempting to smuggle a rooster for cock-fighting.
Packages of cocaine are found concealed in the chassis of a bus, officers receive a tip-off about a vehicle loaded with cartel cash, and a magician comes under scrutiny.
Guy Martin and a team from the Black Country Living Museum restore a Newcomen engine, an 18th-century steam-powered device used principally to pump water out of mines.
A good drive spoiled? Jeremy, Richard and James find out if you can go motor racing for less than it costs to play golf, and rock icon Slash is let loose in a Reasonably Priced Car.
Gear up for more fun with the motor-mad motormouths. Jeremy builds a car that's even smaller than the famous Peel P50, and Damian Lewis is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programmes.show_name: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programmes.show_description: On the Cesme Peninsula, Rick Stein enjoys creamy Armola cheese, Urla's famous red wine, and the ultimate Turkish breakfast.
Breakfast: The Hairy Bikers look for great ways to kick start the day. They find out about how our morning meal has changed and evolved through the years.
Celebrity guests Alex Jones, Chris McCausland, Su Pollard and Rav Wilding reveal extraordinary stories about themselves, but are they telling the truth or making it all up?
TV presenter Gemma Cairney, comedy actor Tim Key, and sports hosts Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling test their general knowledge skills with the ever-genial Pointless host this time.
TV presenter Charlotte Hawkins and comedians David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, and Sophie Duker test themselves against the best-selling author and Pointless friend's fiendish games.
Upsadaisy: Sandi Toksvig hosts an 'upsadaisy' episode and is joined by Justin Moorhouse, Emmanuel Sonubi, Holly Walsh and Alan Davies.
New. Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe and Katherine Ryan as he continues to try to make sense of the bewildering world we live in.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Guest host Adil Ray and panellists Richard Osman and Jess Phillips MP join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.
Dara O Briain, Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons toast ten years of satirical sideswipes with guests Katherine Ryan and Josh Widdicombe. Sepp Blatter provides ample material.
The regulars plus Ray Winstone, Josh Widdicombe, Rachel Riley and Ricky Tomlinson make Yule fools out of each other by mixing festive fibs with facts. Top panel show laughs.
Underthings: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Maisie Adam, Daliso Chaponda, Jack Dee and Alan Davies for a look at some underthings.
Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe and Katherine Ryan as he continues to try to make sense of the bewildering world we live in.
Rylan Clark-Neal, Anton Du Beke, Carol Vorderman, Ed Gamble and Ellie Taylor meet at a restaurant for a fancy meal - and one of them will have to foot the bill for everyone's dinner.
Tom Davis, Julian Clary, Laura Whitmore, Rachel Johnson and Chris Kamara are the celebrities aiming to avoid footing the bill for a very expensive meal out in this episode.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Nation of Gardeners: The bikers celebrate British food. They head into the garden, where through good times and bad our skills have inspired amazing dishes.
Britain's most talented detectives tackle a dangerous arsonist in Bristol, a drug smuggling operation in Manchester, and a family committing shocking animal cruelty in Cheshire.
Special police task forces tackle a brutal stabbing of a 16-year-old boy in Preston two days before Christmas, and Northamptonshire police target a drug-dealing gang on an estate.
To the Finish Line: The construction of a 130-tonne locomotive in Newcastle is nearing the finish line, and an overnight crew must get a Brisbane train on track before the morning commute.

programmes.show_name: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programmes.show_description: Rick Stein's culinary odyssey ends in Istanbul, where he arrives in time for the bluefish season and catches a few himself while fishing on the Bosphorus.
A shipment of cocaine is discovered in the fifth wheel of a tractor-trailer, and officers searching a courier van find a cache of firearms.
Officers in Eagle Pass seize over 15 kilograms of heroin and unveil a new cartel drug-smuggling tactic, and a Mexican family in Laredo makes a plea for humanitarian parole.
Guy Martin helps with the restoration of the oldest surviving Brixham sailing trawler and discovers how Britain acquired its taste for fish and chips.
The boys race three front-engined supercars on an epic trip starting in Las Vegas - last one to the Mexican border is a rotten egg! Fleetwood Mac drummer Mick Fleetwood guests.
The boys' competitive streak rears its head as they race from Wembley to Milan - Clarkson is in a Shelby Mustang GT500 and Hammond and May are on a train. Amy MacDonald guests.

Sorting the fact from fiction with Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell this time are guests Lucy Beaumont, Frankie Boyle, Abby Cook and Mo Gilligan.
TV presenter Gemma Cairney, comedy actor Tim Key, and sports hosts Gabby Logan and Jeff Stelling test their general knowledge skills with the ever-genial Pointless host this time.
TV presenter Charlotte Hawkins and comedians David Baddiel, Alex Brooker, and Sophie Duker test themselves against the best-selling author and Pointless friend's fiendish games.
Underthings: Sandi Toksvig is joined by Maisie Adam, Daliso Chaponda, Jack Dee and Alan Davies for a look at some underthings.
New. Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe and Katherine Ryan as he does stand-up, reviews, discussion and audience interaction in an attempt to make sense of what's going on around us.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Guest host Clive Myrie, journalist Katy Balls, and comedian Jo Brand join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.
